reasoning: The value on 2025-11-09 will likely be 0.84 (the value published on Friday, 2025-11-07). The corporate bond spread index recently spiked from 0.75 in late October to 0.84 in early November. This sudden local peak suggests a high likelihood of short-term mean reversion (tightening back toward 0.80-0.82), making an increase above 0.84 by Nov 16 relatively unlikely (around 36%, anchored by the statistical model estimate of 35%). As the horizon extends to Dec 9 and Feb 2026, the variance increases, and historically low spreads (around 70-80 bps) have an asymmetric risk of widening toward longer-term averages, pushing the probabilities closer to 0.5. However, since the broader macroeconomic trend has supported tight spreads due to strong corporate fundamentals and 'soft landing' consensus, I remain slightly below 50% for the longer dates.
